replies.author: @magus_series
replies.text: @T@TurnerTolkien2013ere’s nothing wrong with caring about your own characters. All of George’s characters are dead and the ones from legends who are supposed to be around at this time weren’t introduced like they should’ve been. As much as a lot of fans would prefer a reset from when George first sold it, Disney did accumulate a lot of extra normie fans since then. Faloni is just working with what he has while still moving forward. He’s taken Ashoka to another galaxy so she’s technically out of the main line. Which should mean anything done with her is her own story and shouldn’t be looked at in major connection to George’s timeline anymore. And Ezra is back in the timeline with Luke, if there’s things to do with Luke we can still get them. It’s all the other useless side quest shows filling you with disappointment. Without the Faloni projects keeping the ship afloat while KK was deliberately trying to sink it, how many fans do you think would still be around.

author: @ronineditor9920
text:
0:53 -- BINGO... this is it.  When you know what you're doing, how a sequence is blocked, what you want without "guessing" on shoot days, you don't go over-budget, into OT for the crew, etc.  And, yup... as you said, George knows what he wants when he directs.  I know he's not the best director for actors... but in his head, the puzzle is there and he's gathering the pieces.  That's critical to keeping things efficient, especially in VFX-heavy films. 

I direct/edit shoots for a studio in 'Hollywood' and my last shoot, the quoted budget was a LOT, by our production team.  I said, no... I want to do it for 1/3 of that.  They thought I was nuts.  It wasn't - I knew EXACTLY the shots I needed since I was going to be editing it.  We got done on time, under budget, and it went on to 100+ million views.  The company was used to things costing more and I'm trying to change the culture, like Filoni is (not that I'm at Disney).  

3:25 -- Not the stages, but the pre-built CGI models, everything rigged already. 

4:50 -- The reality is that certain films like Star Wars, Marvel, etc... have a built-in audience that, no matter what it looks like, how good or bad it is, they're showing up.  The demo you have to target with the marketing is the 'maybe I'll go' crowd so it has to look good enough for them.  Point is, you can spend $200 million more and that might only turn into an extra $20 million at the box office so it's not worth it.  The main thing is getting the story right.  George's films obviously had solid VFX but the key to it all was the story, which is why films in that era like Blackhole tanked, when they shared some of the same crew.

Also, think about the mess that was The Marvels... 2nd time director, in way over her head, mess of a story, way too many reshoots, it was a disaster behind the scenes with the director bailing on her film, and the budget was just under Endgame's budget. 

You CAN tell a great story without an insane budget.

author: @jacobtarter2237
text:
Adjusted for inflation: (I used the us inflation calculator website)

A  new hope cost 59 mill 
Empire strikes back cost 71 mill
Return of the Jedi was 105 mill 
Phantom menace cost 224 mill 
Attack of the clones cost 207 mill
Revenge of the Sith cost 188 mill

Force awakens cost 336 mill to inflation 
Rogue one 271 mill 
TLJ cost 420 mill 
Solo cost 356 mill (counting rewrites prob woulda been much closer to 600-700 mill)
TROS cost 349 mill 


It’s crazy how far budgets have came to where if u adjust for inflation, return of the Jedi would’ve had a similar inflation budget to blue beetle or the sonic movies (which are enjoyable) hell there’s animated movies now that cost more then return of the Jedi w/inflation

replies.author: @jacobtarter2237
replies.text: There’s tv shows that have much bigger budgets then the original trilogy of movies did. Hell season 4 of stranger things had a budget per episode cost of 30-40 million per episode, almost as much as the adjusted for inflation budget of a new hope (and ST season 5 budget per episode was prob closer to empire strikes back inflation budget )

replies.text:
​@DimitrisNtomprosJames Gunns superman was done cheap. Look at the costumes, the CGI, the cast, now compare to Snyders more expensive MOS and it's clear which is better. 

The whole reason why companies started going for CGI over practical or why animators went from hand drawn to CGI is cost. This ks why older films are considered better than nowadays slop because they cut corners n go for the cheapest option rather than the best. 

Even George Lucas's OT Star Wars wouldve cost more than this if it was made nowadays.

The issue is companies are profit orientated n so thats all they care about n they'll do anything to try n achieve that, even at the expense of the consumer n product

replies.author: @BIGDaddioJohnB
replies.text:
So we agree that the issue is the writing and not the money put into it?

The thing is, no matter how much you throw at a turd, it's still a turd. 

Sure, small budget projects such as fan movies can be good because they are made with love, but quality wise, they are still notably low quality compared to high budget hollywood films. If someone like Star Wars Theory was given the same money, then it'd probably be a success because it's actually what fans want.

Money matters. This is why Star Wars Theory went from Live Action to animation on his Vader project because it's cheaper, even though the 1st is better.

author: @TurnerTolkien2013
text: My Grogu and Pedro Pascal fatigue levels are over 9,000, and same with most except seal clapping shills and casuals. Some people tend to forget that S2 was released in late 2020, during the peak of the pandemic, when people were forced to stay inside and binge watch stuff. Lots of those shill channels got more than half of their subscribers because of the pandemic, and now they hardly get any views on their channels. Kyle Katarn is the perfect example. Then after S2 which should've been the Series Finale, they fired Gina, Pedro Pascal showed his true colors and became unbearable, they nerfed and sidelined Boba Fett in his own show, and then nerfed and sidelined Din Djarin in Season 3 so another powerful female character Bo Karen can be Mandalore, while it seems like Boba no longer exists to Filoni and the clowns at Lucasfilm. Then throw in the failures of Kenobi, TBOBF, Mando S3, Ahsoka, and The Acolyte. Honestly, I have no intention of watching any Disney Star Wars content again. I'm happily sticking with franchises like LOTR/Tolkien, Dragon Ball, and One Piece these days. Star Wars will never amount to the greatness of those 3 franchises again.

author: @PLF...
text:
Maybe it's just because Mandalorian doesn't need to cost more than that... They've spend 3 seasons of the show perfecting everything about those characters, and a movie is 2½ hours max (probably not going to be more than 2 hours and 20). Same reason they pump out animated movies once one does well - you've already built everything in a computer, so making the characters just do some more stuff is not very expensive...

Plus, it's probably going to be a lot more down to earth than the more trope-weary Star Wars grand epics, it's like a buddy cop road movie. None of that screams huge set pieces - as a matter of fact, it screams the exact opposite. Cozy sets (without being crammed obviously) and character driven story. So relies mostly on story - and Dave has had what 5-6 years figuring that part out? I think the movie might surprise people expecting Avatar-level over-the-top action visuals for no reason, but it will probably be remembered as a great movie. For what it is. Which is literally in the title - it's about those two.

author: @sandramiller1988
text:
What matters isn’t the budget, it’s the story. 

If they’ve got a great story, a great cast and a great director then I’m in.

I’ll give John Favre the benefit of the doubt for now…

I’m not gonna doom a project before I actually see it in theaters. 

Trailers can be deceptive. 

There have been many movies I hated the trailer and thought the movie would bomb and went to watch it and the film was completely the opposite.

If I don’t like it afterwards, I will definitely say so, but I’m not gonna be one of these people that want something to fail because you’re mad at something else Disney did. 

I don’t want any project to fail. 

I want them all to be great, beautiful and entertaining. 

Unfortunately, they don’t always turn out that way, but I wish for the best…

author: @alexandrostheodwris5004
text: they should change the name to star was instead of star wars

author: @isaaclaughter
text: "This movie also represents a new strategy for Lucasfilm. A lower-budget theatrical release means the financial bar for success is much lower. It doesn't need to chase billion-dollar box office numbers to be considered a win, and once its theater run ends, it's practically guaranteed to perform well on streaming."  OK, but this is AFTER the FACT.  Fact: Disney could have just made great films from jump but instead chose to make a catalog equivalent to a steaming pile.  Of course they have to use this strategy.

text: So it needs about $550M to break even then.  That's a mark I don't think they'll hit, I'm betting on a $480M global boxoffice.

text:
